title: Herian I'ngre

'''Herian I'ngre''' was a [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] who flew [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] with [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
The [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] Herian I'ngre joined the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic's]] elite [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] [[Squadron/Legends|unit]] [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]]<ref name="X-Wing Handbook">[[Star Wars Handbook 1: X-Wing Rogue Squadron|''Star Wars Handbook'' 1: ''X-Wing Rogue Squadron'']]</ref> seven [[Standard month/Legends|months]] after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="Rogue Squadron Omnibus Volume 2">''[[Star Wars Omnibus: X-Wing Rogue Squadron Volume 2]]''</ref> As a Bith, she went against her [[Species/Legends|species']] noted pacifistic tendencies, specifically due to the species diversity of the New Republic compared to the [[Humanocentrism|Humanocentric]] chauvinism of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. Due to her species' characteristic short-sightedness, I'ngre wore corrective flight goggles while flying.

Shortly after she joined the squadron, [[Rogue Leader/Legends|Rogue Leader]] [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]] assigned her to rig her fellow [[Pilot/Legends|pilots']] seats with electrical charges during a [[Flight simulator/Legends|simulation]], in order to produce more realism.

I'ngre joined the squadron in time for the [[Eiattu Campaign|mission]] to [[Eiattu 6]]. The Rogues mostly [[Ground warfare/Legends|fought on the ground]] during that campaign, battling the forces of [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Leonia Tavira]]. I'ngre was captured along with several of her comrades, but she was rescued thanks to the efforts of the Rogue [[Nrin Vakil]].

I'ngre's second mission with Rogue Squadron took her to [[Malrev IV]] in an effort to rescue the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Passenger liner/Legends|spaceliner]] ''[[Starfaring]]''. Upon arriving on the planet the Rogues were [[Malrev incident|attacked]] by [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] piloted by primitive [[Irrukiine]], who were under the control of the sorcerer [[Cartariun]]'s [[Sith sorcery/Legends|Sith magic]].

[[File:Herian_firing_torpedoes.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Herian's X-wing starfighter fires proton torpedoes into the Sith temple.]]
That proved to be I'ngre's only true fighter engagement with the squadron, as she was gravely wounded the next [[Standard day/Legends|day]] during a ground battle with Carturian's forces. She suffered [[Brain/Legends|brain]] damage, and the goggles she used to fly were destroyed, grounding her. But when her squadron was in danger of being swamped by the enemy, I'ngre stole fellow Rogue [[Feylis Ardele]]'s damaged fighter and, with [[Dllr Nep]]'s guidance, flew the ship into the [[Sith Temple (Malrev IV)|Sith temple]] responsible for the carnage on the planet. I'ngre and Nep sacrificed themselves to destroy the temple and save the lives of their fellow pilots.

I'ngre was known to possess an interest in [[Music/Legends|music]], often discussing the techniques of different bands with Nep, the Squadron's other music fan.

==Personality and traits==
I'ngre was vocally self-reflective and inquisitive, having logic and intellect tinged with innocence and wonder. She defined heroism as an intellectual and emotional subjugation of the most basic primal desire for self-preservation. I'ngre had [[Color/Legends|pink]] skin, black [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and, like most of her [[Species/Legends|species]], was short-sighted.<ref name="CSWE" />
